nodes.context: Mafia-type criminal organisation in Calabria, Italy.
A powerful 'ndrina within the 'Ndrangheta that rose during the First 'Ndrangheta war.
A traditional 'ndrina that favored La Santa and modernizing the 'Ndrangheta.
A traditional 'ndrina opposed to new developments like drug trafficking.
Camorra organization led by Raffaele Cutolo, involved in drug trafficking with the De Stefanos.
Clan that helped kill Antonio Macrì.
Clan that helped kill Antonio Macrì.
Cosca that supported the De Stefano clan in the aftermath.

edges.evidence_quote: Tripodo tried to strike first, killing Giovanni De Stefano and wounding his brother Giorgio De Stefano on 24 November 1974, in the Roof Garden bar, an infamous 'Ndrangheta haunt in Reggio Calabria.
edges.context: The Tripodo group attacked the De Stefano clan, starting the conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: He was killed by Giuseppe Suraci, apparently out of personal vengeance, but in reality on the orders of Piromalli, who feared the rise of the De Stefanos.
edges.context: Piromalli ordered the killing of Giorgio De Stefano, indicating conflict between the two groups.

edges.evidence_quote: On 26 August 1976, he was stabbed to death in prison on the request of the De Stefanos with the help of Camorra boss Raffaele Cutolo, the boss of the Nuova Camorra Organizzata (NCO) who worked with the De Stefanos in drug trafficking.
edges.context: The De Stefano clan cooperated with the NCO to kill Tripodo and in drug trafficking.

edges.evidence_quote: Macrì was killed with the help of the Cataldo and Mazzaferro clans.
edges.context: The Cataldo clan helped the De Stefano clan kill Macrì.

edges.evidence_quote: Macrì was killed with the help of the Cataldo and Mazzaferro clans.
edges.context: The Mazzaferro clan helped the De Stefano clan kill Macrì.

edges.evidence_quote: They won a monopoly of construction work in northern Reggio Calabria, moving the rival Tripodo group out of the market of public work contracts with the support of the Piromalli and Mammoliti cosche.
edges.context: The Mammoliti cosca supported the De Stefano clan in the aftermath of the war.
